item.title: Comer threatens to subpoena Dershowitz after he refuses Epstein testimony
item.description: Alan Dershowitz backed out of a voluntary interview, prompting James Comer to warn the House Oversight Committee will pursue compulsory process.
item.content:encoded:
Harvard Law School professor Alan Dershowitz is facing renewed pressure from the powerful House Oversight Committee after refusing to testify before its bipartisan probe into Jeffrey Epstein and his longtime associate, Ghislaine Maxwell.

House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer, R-Ky., warned Tuesday the panel would pursue "compulsory" action if Dershowitz does not sit for a transcribed video interview with congressional investigators in early September.

The letter comes after Dershowitz, who served as Epstein's defense lawyer during his controversial 2008 plea deal involving the solicitation of a minor for prostitution, backed out of a voluntary transcribed interview slated for July 20.

"On July 15, 2026, your counsel notified the Committee that you ‘cannot now testify in Congress concerning the matters in which [the Committee] are interested," Comer wrote in the letter. "Your counsel failed to provide a reason for your refusal to testify."

'SHE SAW DOLLAR SIGNS': FORMER OBAMA COUNSEL PRESSED OVER YEARS-LONG EPSTEIN TIES

"When Committee staff followed up with your counsel via email on July 15 and 17, 2026, your counsel again failed to provide an explanation for your refusal to attend your transcribed interview," the Kentucky Republican added. "This is unacceptable."

Comer is requesting that Dershowitz now participate in a videotaped transcribed interview on Sept. 2.

"The Committee will not tolerate any further delay," he added. "If you fail to adhere to this request, the Committee will explore all available tools at its disposal, including the use of the compulsory process."

Dershowitz has recently expressed interest in testifying about his ties to Epstein — a point Comer repeatedly points to in the letter. He has denied any wrongdoing.

DEMOCRAT CALLS HOWARD LUTNICK A 'PATHOLOGICAL LIAR' AFTER CLOSED-DOOR EPSTEIN TESTIMONY

"I’m very proud of my role in representing Epstein," Dershowitz said during an interview with NewsNation in June. "I’m prepared to testify about everything. I offered to testify, volunteered for the past few months. I will personally not invoke privilege. I have nothing to hide."

But he told The Harvard Crimson last week that his attorneys told him that he could not testify to the committee, citing unspecified legal prohibitions.

Comer's letter comes as a bipartisan group of lawmakers has subpoenaed several high-profile individuals to testify before the committee as part of its yearlong probe, including former Attorney General Pam Bondi, former President Bill Clinton and his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.

Other high-profile figures who knew Epstein have recently participated in voluntary interviews with the committee, including tech billionaire Bill Gates, former Obama White House counsel Kathryn Ruemmler and one-time Clinton aide Doug Band.

item.title: Pentagon identifies Army soldier killed in action during Iraq operation
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton was killed during a controlled detonation of a downed Iranian attack drone at Erbil Air Base, raising the U.S. toll to 17.
item.content:encoded:
The Army on Tuesday identified Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, from Fayetteville, North Carolina, as the U.S. service member killed while troops attempted to destroy a downed Iranian attack drone Sunday at an air base in Iraq.

Swinton, a U.S. Army Space and Missile Defense Command (USASMDC) soldier, was killed in action during a controlled detonation of the one-way unmanned aerial system at Erbil Air Base, the Department of War announced. The incident remains under investigation.

"Every time Iran kills an American Soldier they will pay for that killing many times over!" Trump wrote Monday on Truth Social. "This directive has been passed on to Secretary of War, Pete Hegseth, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Daniel Caine, and every Leader in the Military."

Swinton's death brought the number of U.S. service members killed since the beginning of the conflict with Iran to 17. The toll includes deaths from Iranian attacks as well as crashes and other incidents connected to American military operations in the region.

US SERVICE MEMBER KILLED IN IRAQ AS TROOPS RECOVER UNIDENTIFIED REMAINS IN JORDAN: CENTCOM

"USASMDC is saddened by the loss of Sgt. Swinton, whose death occurred during an operation to safeguard fellow soldiers," Lt. Gen. John L. Rafferty, USASMDC commanding general, wrote in a statement. "His service will not be forgotten, and his sacrifice is not in vain. We will support Sgt. Swinton's family and his unit during this difficult period. His loss will inspire his fellow soldiers to continue the mission."

U.S. Central Command previously said the unexploded ordnance came from a downed Iranian one-way attack drone. Another service member was injured during the detonation and received medical treatment.

Swinton was assigned to D Battery, 2nd Battalion, 55th Air Defense Artillery Regiment, 108th Air Defense Artillery Brigade, based at Fort Bragg, North Carolina.

"Sgt. Swinton was an experienced noncommissioned officer who took great pride in his work and the people around him," Brig. Gen. John L. Dawber said in a statement. "He answered the call to duty with courage, honor, and selfless dedication."

ARMY IDS 2 VICTIMS KILLED IN JORDAN AIR BASE ATTACK; TRUMP: IRAN WILL PAY 'MANY TIMES OVER'

Swinton, who will receive the Bronze Star, Purple Heart and Combat Action Badge, and be posthumously promoted to staff sergeant, had already received the Army Commendation Medal, Army Achievement Medal, Army Good Conduct Medal, National Defense Service Medal, Global War on Terrorism Expeditionary Medal, Army Service Ribbon and Combat Action Badge.

His death followed the killing of two U.S. soldiers in Jordan on Friday – 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas – as American and allied forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks.

Central Command said another service member remained missing after those attacks, with President Donald Trump suspecting that missing member to have died, too.

TWO US SERVICE MEMBERS KILLED IN IRANIAN STRIKES ON JORDAN, CENTCOM SAYS

"We hit them very hard again tonight, and we did that in honor of the probably three – it's probably three as opposed to two great patriots," Trump told reporters at Joint Base Andrews on Sunday night.

"We feel very badly, but you know, those great people, those great patriots were out there fighting that Iran could not have a nuclear weapon."

The United States launched Operation Epic Fury against Iran on Feb. 28. U.S. and partner forces have since targeted Iranian air defenses, missile and drone facilities, maritime capabilities and other military infrastructure in a restarting of strikes after the Iranian violations of the memorandum of understanding (MOU) over its fight for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

More than 50,000 U.S. military personnel are operating across the Middle East, according to Central Command.

Fox News' James Levinson contributed to this report.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Viral German fan Freddy welcomed to White House with tomahawks steaks, American muscle & MAHA fries
item.description: Freddy, the viral German World Cup fan, visits the White House as Nick Adams plans a tomahawk steak lunch and West Wing tour before his flight home.
item.content:encoded:
Before he packs his bags and bids farewell to the United States, the internet's favorite German World Cup fan has one more high-profile destination to check off his list and it's a big one.

The fan, known as Freddy — who rocketed to stardom so fast during the World Cup that he was hanging out with country star Ella Langley and sitting in the Orion space capsule between German soccer matches — is visiting the White House Tuesday, Special Presidential Envoy and United States "Tourism Minister" Nick Adams tells OutKick.

"From the rolling plains and towering skylines to the unbreakable spirit of her people, America doesn't just welcome visitors, she embraces them like family, feeds them like kings, and sends them home forever changed," Ambassador Adams said in a statement provided to OutKick. "As the President's Special Envoy and the first-ever Minister of Tourism for these United States, I am honored to showcase the real America: bold, bountiful, and built on strength, freedom, and generosity that knows no equal."

REPUBLICAN LEADERS EMBRACE VIRAL WORLD CUP FANS THEY SAY ARE DISCOVERING THE 'REAL AMERICA'

Freddy, who is expected to fly home Thursday, is in for a treat to close out his trip.

Instead of forcing Freddy to hop in a weed smoke-filled Uber to ride over to the White House from Crystal City, Adams promises Freddy won't have to deal with some degenerate chauffeur. "The day's unforgettable itinerary will begin with a pickup in a hulking, American-made black SUV, the type of vehicle where passengers feel like the undisputed king of the road," Adams explains. "He will be greeted with sweeping views from the Speaker's Balcony of the U.S. Capitol, where the grand architecture of our Republic stands as a monument to liberty and resolve. It will continue with an exclusive monuments tour led by the Department of the Interior, illuminating the storied landmarks that tell the epic tale of American triumph."

From there, the German social media star will be treated to lunch unlike any he's had so far in the States. We're talking a tomahawk steak with a side of "golden MAHA-approved beef tallow fries, crisp onion rings, and hearty bone-in chicken wings—all paired with the coldest domestic beer our capital has to offer. This meal will be a celebration of the American ranchers and farmers who fuel our greatness," Adams continues.

FREDDY, THE VIRAL WORLD CUP TOURIST, LINKS UP WITH COUNTRY SINGER ELLA LANGLEY AND THE INTERNET LOVES IT

Will Freddy meet with President Trump? That's still to be determined, but Adams promises he'll take our German guest on a tour of the West Wing where he can rub elbows with top White House executives.

"Driving through Ohio. Towns like this are why we’re trying to avoid the interstates and take the slower roads instead. These are the kinds of places we want to see. This feels like peak America," Freddy wrote in June of his experience traveling through Elmore, Ohio, a sleepy, but prideful village of 1,600 residents.

That video was watched 12 million times on X and caused an outpouring of emotions.

GERMAN WORLD CUP FAN BREAKS DOWN IN TEARS AFTER BEING BLOWN AWAY BY KINDNESS OF AMERICANS

"The folks on the coasts call the area you are in fly-over states as if they’re of no value. I’m so glad you’re going thru some of the Midwest and seeing the small-town hearts of America," X user Kristin Johnson wrote to Freddy.

She wasn't alone in praise.

"This is literally probably one of the first posts about Ohio I’ve seen on the internet that’s not making fun of it. Kudos," a gamer dad named Tom Games added.

Five days prior to the Ohio visit, Freddy slipped on a pair of cowboy boots for a special occasion. The boots were a gift from Ella Langley and her team.

"This is the most American I‘ve ever felt," he tweeted. That night, Freddy and his buddies met Langley backstage after a performance in Oklahoma City.

Today, the road trip of a lifetime comes to an end at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue, where America's favorite German fan gets the ultimate red-carpet sendoff from the heart of the Free World.

item.title: WATCH: Weingarten gestures wildly during anti-Trump rant, later says he drives her to drink
item.description: Randi Weingarten mocked Trump's White House UFC event and called his tactics authoritarian during her fiery AFT conference keynote speech.
item.content:encoded:
The American Federation of Teachers conference in Washington turned into an anti-Trump spectacle as union boss Rhonda "Randi" Weingarten repeatedly leaped on stage during her keynote speech and later quipped that President Donald Trump has her reaching for vodka.

Weingarten, a longtime foe of the president and top Republicans, opened her keynote speech by mocking the White House's Independence Day cage match and later suggested that the president is an authoritarian whose daily stream of political actions drives her to drink.

In welcoming teachers and activists from around the country, Weingarten quipped: "While you missed the UFC cage fight on the White House lawn, it’s not too late to check out the tire marks in the Reflecting Pool."

TRUMP'S BOLD PREDICTION FOR HISTORIC UFC WHITE HOUSE FIGHT REVEALED IN NEW FOX NATION DOCUMENTARY

During a panel discussion that included former Obama Attorney General Eric Holder, Weingarten asked the panelists what keeps them up at night with many things "confronting the country."

"Because part of what authoritarians do — and also part of what Donald Trump does, whether you consider him an authoritarian or not — is the throwing out of so much stuff that people just throw up their hands and say: ‘Oh no, what are we going to do? All I want to do is stay home and’ — for those of us who drink — ‘have a glass of wine’ — or for me, some vodka."

The Clarkstown, New York, native went on to recount a conference speech by Sen. Raphael Warnock, D-Ga., who referenced a snake convulsing as it neared its death.

FAR-LEFT GROUPS PIVOT FROM MAY DAY PROTESTS TO MOBILIZE VOTERS FOR 2026 MIDTERMS, PUSHING DEMS LEFTWARD

"It looks really scary, but that is a convulsion nearing death. And it’s been something I thought about this morning waking up, because if you think about how Trump looked yesterday," Weingarten said, going on to note the volume of union members living in swing states ahead of the midterms.

Fox News Digital reached out to the White House for comment.

During the keynote, Weingarten name-checked several conservative entities focused on school choice, from reporter Christopher Rufo to the Heritage Foundation.

FAR-LEFT ACTIVIST CONFERENCE AT TEACHERS UNION HQ RAISES CLASSROOM CONCERNS

Weingarten grew increasingly animated as she pivoted from Trump and purported "oligarchs" like Elon Musk to her confidence that the AFT would come out in force and bring about a leftward policy shift at the polls.

"Yes," she boomed. "The other side has power."

"But this is what they don’t have: the power of the people," she said as she began to jump.

"That is not a mere slogan. It is the potential to work together, to bargain together, to protest together, to vote together, to act together, to bring about change — real, lasting, life-improving change.

That is the power and potential of our movement and of the A – F – T," she shouted, elongating her organization’s acronym.

Weingarten later said she wanted to see an America built where "the trans nurse, the Palestinian teacher, the Jewish student and every person, regardless of race, religion, ethnicity, gender or place of origin, feel welcome, safe and valued."

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

She said the AFT can use its power for "good" against "corruption" and said that when they fight they bring a better life for all.

"And one more thing: We get s--- done," she said, before footnoting: "My apologies to the children."

Fox News Digital reached out to AFT for additional comment.

Fox News Digital's Hannah Brennan contributed to this report.

item.title: WATCH: Pelosi dodges questions on husband's new hit-and-run charge
item.description: Nancy Pelosi refused to answer questions about Paul Pelosi's misdemeanor hit-and-run charge and her vote to slash $3.3 billion in aid to Israel.
item.content:encoded:
Former Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., remained silent when repeatedly pressed about her husband's newly filed misdemeanor hit-and-run charge, declining to answer whether she believed the charge was fair.

"Can you tell us if you think the misdemeanor charge against Paul was fair?" Fox News Digital asked Pelosi as she walked to her car, refusing to respond.

Paul Pelosi faces a maximum sentence of up to six months in county jail, a fine of up to $1,000 or both. In addition to the misdemeanor filed Friday, Pelosi was also charged with an infraction for making an unsafe turn in the incident on July 3.

PAUL PELOSI, 86, FACES A HIT-AND-RUN CHARGE AFTER STRIKING A PARKED CAR IN NAPA COUNTY: SHERIFF'S OFFICE

The charge filed by the Napa County District Attorney’s Office come after the 86-year-old broke California law by allegedly not making any attempt to contact or identify the owner of a parked Tesla that he damaged.

As Pelosi drove away in his Maserati convertible, his car broke down after driving about a half mile. Deputies found Pelosi, and he claimed that he had known he hit something, but was unsure of what and had planned to return to the crash scene.

The incident prompted authorities to request Pelosi undergo a behind-the-wheel driving evaluation to ensure he is able to safely operate a vehicle.

PELOSI STAFFER PUMPS THE BRAKES WHEN PRESSED ON HUSBAND'S HIT-AND-RUN: 'THAT'S ENOUGH'

This is not the first time Pelosi has gained national attention for an unlawful driving incident. In 2022, he pleaded guilty to driving under the influence and crashing into an oncoming Jeep, injuring the driver and was charged with a misdemeanor.

It was also reported by The New York Times that Pelosi was behind the wheel in the 1957 incident that killed his 19-year-old brother, though he was cleared by a jury for any wrongdoing in the case.

HOUSE DEMOCRATS FRACTURE BADLY OVER MASSIE AMENDMENT TO CUT $3.3B IN U.S. AID TO ISRAEL

Fox News Digital also asked the Democratic former speaker about her recent vote — alongside over 100 House Democrats — for an amendment introduced by Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky., to cut $3.3 billion in aid to Israel.

The amendment failed 314-104, with Massie being the only Republican to vote in its favor, but it showed the pressure on the Democratic Party to take a stance against Israel and its handling of the war in Gaza.

It also represents the changing view of Democrats’ stance on Israel, particularly under the Trump administration and as Israel’s Prime Minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, has been under fire for his actions toward Gaza.

Primary elections have also reflected American voters’ outrage with Israel, as the nation has seen progressive candidates who have spoken out against Israel and in favor of Palestinians sweep victories over incumbents and mainstream Democrats as the elected nominees for the November midterms.

item.title: Fleeing suspect captured after wild Range Rover chase across Los Angeles County
item.description: Video shows the black Range Rover weaving through traffic on the 101 Freeway before CHP officers rammed the SUV to end the chase in Montebello.
item.content:encoded:
A suspect on the move was taken into custody Tuesday morning after leading California Highway Patrol officers on a high-speed chase through Los Angeles County.

Live video of the chase aired on FOX 11 Los Angeles.

WATCH: FELONY SUSPECT'S OCEAN GETAWAY UNRAVELS AFTER 90-MINUTE STANDOFF WITH LIFEGUARDS

The incident began around 7:15 a.m. when Santa Monica Police Department investigators attempted to take the driver of a black Range Rover, who was allegedly involved in a road rage incident a couple of weeks ago, into custody, according to police.

The driver exited the freeway into Koreatown and continued speeding through surface streets, at times crossing into opposing lanes and endangering morning commuters.

2 COLOMBIAN NATIONALS ARRESTED IN STOLEN MERCEDES LINKED TO VIOLENT CALIFORNIA CARJACKING

The Range Rover reportedly reached speeds exceeding 100 mph as the chase entered the San Gabriel Valley shortly before 8 a.m.

The pursuit ended in Montebello after the driver traveled into a center median and a trailing patrol vehicle rammed the SUV. Multiple officers surrounded the Range Rover and took the suspect into custody, according to the report.

Authorities have not identified the suspect or disclosed details about why he was being pursued.

item.title: Whitney Cummings claims lurid Trump joke was cut from Netflix's Mark Twain Prize special
item.description: Whitney Cummings alleged a joke she made about President Donald Trump was cut by Netflix from its Mark Twain Prize special honoring Bill Maher.
item.content:encoded:
Comedian Whitney Cummings is saying that one of her jokes about President Donald Trump was cut from Netflix's Mark Twain Prize special honoring Bill Maher.

Cummings, who was among those who paid tribute to the "Real Time" host at the Kennedy Center ceremony last month, made headlines with an edgy jab about the president, telling the audience, "I actually heard Trump may come tonight, but he couldn’t make it. He got caught in sex traffic."

Cummings said her routine was meddled with.

"Not only did I have to cut a joke in rehearsal, then the joke I did — I ended on 'Donald Trump couldn't make it tonight, he got caught in sex traffic' — it stayed in, and then they said, 'We would like to cut it out,'" Cummings said on Saturday's installment of her "Good for You" podcast.

BILL MAHER SAYS IT’S 'SO STUPID' FOR ANTI-TRUMP ARTISTS TO BAIL ON KENNEDY CENTER PERFORMANCES

She clarified the Kennedy Center staff as being "amazing" and said they "did their job," noting that Trump was their boss since he was made chair of the Kennedy Center board.

"I was presented with a tricky conundrum. I just don't know if I did the right thing or not," Cummings said. "It was very clear to me that they would lose their jobs."

"The joke was already leaked by someone that was in the audience... but then they said we need to cut it from the broadcast. And I'm like, 'Well it's already out there. Who cares?' And they're like, 'He will sue you.' Which is like, I don't buy it. I don't buy it, No. 1. No. 2, he might," Cummings chuckled before referencing Trump's infamous 2013 lawsuit against Maher after he joked Trump was the son of an "orangutan."

FEDERAL JUDGE ORDERS TRUMP'S NAME REMOVED FROM KENNEDY CENTER, SAYS ONLY CONGRESS CAN RENAME IT

She said she felt the need to fight for the joke because it was the Mark Twain Prize.

"This is the only time that you shouldn't get censored, because it is about comedians using humor to criticize our elected officials," she said. "So I was in this jam where I was like... I need to fight for this joke because it's the Mark Twain Prize. And this is the time I feel like I have to, but also I don't want anyone losing their job."

Cummings went on to shrug off the potential threat of a Trump lawsuit against her, confidently saying, "I would win."

Netflix pointed the finger at the Trump-controlled Kennedy Center, telling Fox News Digital, "While it’s our preference not to cut jokes from anyone’s set, The Kennedy Center has the contractual right to do so."

"Edits to a show are always made in a collaborative way to produce the best product for broadcast," a spokesperson for The Kennedy Center told Fox News Digital about its partnership with Netflix. 

In a previous podcast, Cummings revealed she was forced to cut another Trump joke from her set, this one pertaining to his "petty" move to add his name to the Kennedy Center and that Trump allegedly "always hated" John F. Kennedy, resulting in a vulgar punchline involving Kennedy's affair with actress Marilyn Monroe.

"I was practicing my jokes and people were like, 'Yeah, we'll get fired if you say that.' I'm like, that is the worst thing you could hear as a comic," Cummings recalled. "I did cut a joke, and I feel the need to just say it."

CLICK HERE FOR THE LATEST MEDIA AND CULTURE NEWS

A Kennedy Center source familiar with the production told Fox News Digital that Cummings' comments have "no validity" and that the center "would never fire anyone for a joke."

They also insist there wasn't foul play when it comes to the Trump joke being cut, saying it's "normal course" for the show to be cut down due to time constraints and that it's "standard practice," pointing to jokes cut last year from its special honoring Conan O'Brien. 

The White House did not immediately respond to Fox News Digital's requests for comment.

The Mark Twain Prize special honoring Maher began streaming on Netflix on Tuesday. The "sex traffic" jab did not appear during Cummings' routine.

item.title: 'Godzilla vs. Kong' actress Kaylee Hottle dead at 18
item.description: Kaylee Hottle, whose performance as Jia brought authentic deaf representation to the "Godzilla" franchise, died Tuesday morning at 18 after a tragic car accident.
item.content:encoded:
Kaylee Hottle has died, Fox News Digital can confirm.

The 18-year-old deaf actress, known for her role in "Godzilla vs. Kong," died Tuesday morning in a car accident.

TMZ reported that her father revealed the crash occurred in Maryland during a nearly 23-minute livestream, explaining in American Sign Language that he flew from Texas to claim Kaylee's body.

HOLLYWOOD STARS WHO DIED IN 2026

Joshua Hottle said he was told Kaylee had been involved in a car accident. Officials later informed him that her heart stopped on the way to the hospital.

Before landing her first feature film role, Kaylee appeared in commercials and advocacy projects promoting deaf awareness.

A Virginia native born into a multigenerational deaf family, she quickly became a rising talent and an advocate for greater representation in entertainment.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT NEWSLETTER

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS

Hottle landed the role of Jia in "Godzilla vs. Kong" in 2021.

She reprised the role three years later in "Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire," reuniting with the franchise as Jia's connection to Kong remained central to the story. The sequel expanded her character's role and further showcased her signing throughout the film.

Her authentic performance as Jia, a deaf girl who shares a unique bond with Kong, earned widespread praise and helped increase representation of deaf actors in major Hollywood productions.

Though her acting career had only recently begun, she left a lasting impact through her performances and her advocacy for greater inclusion in Tinseltown.
